examples: What inspired the Paleolithic artists to make such beautiful art in such inaccessible places?
Getting to the paintings themselves, virtually all Paleolithic cave art represents animals, and Chauvet is no exception.
The earliest discovered traces of art are beads and carvings, and then paintings, from sites dating back to the Upper Paleolithic period.
The knife--originally knapped stone--has been the partner of the needle since the Paleolithic era.
The art of storytelling has been around since the beginning of time, from the Lascaux Paleolithic cave paintings through Homer and Shakespeare.
